title: Connectors Runtime Manifest
description: Runtime artifact at ~/.claude/connectors/manifest.json — the materialized connector wiring consumed by the connector-runtime cron-wrapper, the SP14 wizard's /connectors status command, and T-13/T-14/T-15 reliability infrastructure. Sourced from user-manifest.json#/connectors at install time, then mutated in-place by per-connector runs (last_run / last_status / auth_status updates). Schema mirrors user-manifest-schema.json#/properties/connectors/items but stands alone so consumers can validate against it without loading the full user-manifest schema.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this runtime schema. 1.0.0 ships with SP14 T-3.

wizard_version.description: SP14 wizard version that produced this manifest. Mirrored from user-manifest.json#/connectors_meta/wizard_version.

last_wizard_run.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of last wizard completion.

connectors.description: Materialized connector entries. Per-entry mutations (last_run, last_status, auth_status) happen here at runtime; user-manifest.json is the seed source-of-truth and is re-read only when /connectors --resync is invoked.

mcp_server.description: MCP server identifier as registered in ~/.claude/settings.json#/mcpServers.

schedule.description: Human-friendly cron-style schedule string. Runtime derives orchestration.json .jobs[] schedule shape from this — see docs/connectors-schema.md for the connector→job derivation contract.

run_count.description: Cumulative successful run count. Maintained by connector-runtime cron-wrapper for T-14 run-history dashboard. Runtime-only — does NOT mirror back to user-manifest.json.

consecutive_failures.description: Run-of-failures counter for the auto-disable threshold (T-15 auth-expiry detector resets to 0 on next successful run). Runtime-only.
